# Numerical noise reference

*Generated by `scripts/measure_op_noise.py`; do not edit by hand.*
*Measured at commit `c234ba4` on `2026-04-29`.*

Every piecewise-linear op in `torchwright/ops/` is measured on one or more
named input distributions characterising its expected input ranges.
The canonical data lives in `docs/op_noise_data.json`; this file is
regenerated from that JSON by `make measure-noise`.

For commentary on the numbers — which are expected, which warrant investigation — see the hand-written `docs/numerical_noise_findings.md`.

## Summary

| Op | Module | Max abs error | Max rel error | Worst distribution |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `abs` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `abs_uniform_pm50` |
| `bool_all_true` | `torchwright.ops.logic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `bool_triple_signed` |
| `bool_any_true` | `torchwright.ops.logic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `bool_triple_signed` |
| `bool_not` | `torchwright.ops.logic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `bool_single_signed` |
| `ceil_int` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `ceil_integers_neg5_10` |
| `clamp` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 1.907e-06 | 0.0002899 | `clamp_flat_zone_pm20` |
| `compare` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 1.998 | 1.998 | `compare_near_thresh_0` |
| `cond_gate` | `torchwright.ops.logic_ops` | 0.0009766 | 0.3885 | `cond_gate_signed_bool_times_value` |
| `equals_vector` | `torchwright.ops.logic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `equals_vector_match_and_off` |
| `exp` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.02797 | 0.0001924 | `exp_pm5` |
| `floor_int` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.9993 | 0.953 | `floor_near_boundary_10` |
| `linear_bin_index` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 1 | 0.9854 | `linear_bin_index_tex_col_16` |
| `log` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.003026 | 0.003721 | `log_6decades_wide` |

| `max` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 3.815e-06 | 1.953e-06 | `minmax_uniform_pm50` |
| `min` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 3.815e-06 | 1.953e-06 | `minmax_uniform_pm50` |

| `piecewise_linear` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.25 | 231.1 | `parabola_0_10_step1` |
| `piecewise_linear_2d` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 7.778 | 0.6024 | `diff_trig_nonuniform` |
| `reciprocal` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.0009137 | 0.1686 | `reciprocal_03_200` |
| `signed_multiply` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.06248 | 2.15 | `signed_multiply_pm10` |
| `square` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.25 | 231.1 | `square_unsigned_0_10` |
| `square_signed` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0.25 | 276.7 | `square_signed_pm10` |
| `thermometer_floor_div` | `torchwright.ops.arithmetic_ops` | 0 | 0 | `thermometer_integers_0_100_by10` |

## How to read these numbers

Each row reports output noise *at the op's design operating point* —
i.e., with clean inputs drawn from the listed distribution. It does
**not** bound output noise when upstream inputs are themselves noisy.

Per-op bounds are **not additive** through a chain when any op has
internal gain (a constant that multiplies an input). Gate ops
(`cond_gate`, `select`, `attend_mean_where`) and threshold-output ops
(`compare` in its ramp zone, `floor_int` near integer boundaries) all
have structural gains > 1. An upstream deviation of magnitude `1/gain`
can push the gated op outside its published bound, and the bias
propagates multiplicatively downstream. See
`docs/numerical_noise_findings.md` for the worked Phase E example and
the list of known amplification hazards.

To reason about a specific chain, don't try to compose per-op bounds.
Probe the suspect node directly with
`torchwright.debug.probe.probe_compiled`; the residual at the node is
observable, the per-op bounds are an aid for spotting the op *class*
most likely responsible.

## `abs`

Exact decomposition: `ReLU(x) + ReLU(-x)`. Any non-zero measurement here would indicate a compiler or float32 bug.

### `abs_uniform_pm50`

Uniform signed inputs on [-50, 50]. `abs` is exact (ReLU(x) + ReLU(-x)) so any residual is float32 round-off only.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=-0.3743` |

## `bool_all_true`

Single `compare` at threshold `N-1` over the sum of N ±1 inputs. Sum is N only when all are +1; otherwise ≤ N-2 — threshold cleanly separates the two, so exact on ±1 inputs.

### `bool_triple_signed`

Three independent ±1 boolean streams — exercises all 8 truth-table rows of `bool_any_true` and `bool_all_true`.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `a=-1`, `b=1`, `c=1` |

## `bool_any_true`

Two stacked `compare` layers. On clean ±1 inputs the intermediate sum lands well outside every ramp zone, so the composition is exact.

### `bool_triple_signed`

Three independent ±1 boolean streams — exercises all 8 truth-table rows of `bool_any_true` and `bool_all_true`.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `a=-1`, `b=1`, `c=1` |

## `bool_not`

Single `compare` with inverted levels. Exact on clean ±1 inputs; inherits `compare`'s ramp-zone error for inputs near 0.

### `bool_single_signed`

Random ±1 inputs (discrete, no ramp-zone samples) — `bool_not` is only defined on clean boolean inputs.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=-1` |

## `ceil_int`

Reduction to `-floor_int(-x)`. On integer inputs every value lands in a flat zone of the floor staircase, so the op is exact here even though it inherits approximate semantics for continuous inputs.

### `ceil_integers_neg5_10`

Integer inputs on [-5, 10]. `ceil_int(k) = k` for integer k (flat zones in the floor staircase reused under -floor(-x)).

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 3834 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=7` |

## `clamp`

Piecewise-linear identity with 4 breakpoints at `[lo, lo + eps, hi - eps, hi]`. In the interior flat zone (this distribution) the op is a pure identity; boundary samples would fall inside the `eps`-wide ramp and lose precision but are out of scope for this negative-control distribution.

### `clamp_flat_zone_pm20`

Uniform samples on [-8, 8] with clamp bounds [-10, 10] — every sample is inside the flat identity zone. Residual measures float32 round-off only.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 1.907e-06 |
| Mean abs error | 2.464e-07 |
| p99 abs error | 9.537e-07 |
| Max rel error | 0.0002899 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 1.99e-07 |
| p99 rel error | 1.661e-06 |
| Worst input | `x=6.46` |

## `compare`

ReLU-pair ramp. Inputs within `1/step_sharpness` of the threshold land inside the ramp zone and yield an interpolated value; inputs further away match the step function exactly. The `compare_near_thresh_*` distributions deliberately stress the ramp — the reference is a discrete step function, so samples inside the ramp legitimately report large error.

### `compare_near_thresh_0`

Samples within ±0.3 of the threshold at 0.0 — stresses the 2-neuron ramp zone (width 1/step_sharpness).

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 1.998 |
| Mean abs error | 0.1679 |
| p99 abs error | 1.901 |
| Max rel error | 1.998 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.1679 |
| p99 rel error | 1.901 |
| Worst input | `x=0.0001239` |

### `compare_uniform_pm80`

Uniform samples on [-80, 80] — measures compare's ramp error well away from the threshold (expected ≈ 0).

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 1.339 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0004795 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 1.339 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.0004795 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=0.03305` |

## `cond_gate`

`big_offset` ReLU gate. On clean ±1 conditions the output is exactly `inp` (cond=+1) or `0` (cond=-1) up to float32 round-off; noisy conditions blur the gate.

### `cond_gate_signed_bool_times_value`

±1 conditions paired with scalar values in [-5, 5]. Expected output: `value` when cond=+1, `0` when cond=-1.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.0009766 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0002494 |
| p99 abs error | 0.0009595 |
| Max rel error | 0.3885 (over 2089 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.001052 |
| p99 rel error | 0.0116 |
| Worst input | `cond=1`, `inp=3.06` |

## `equals_vector`

Single-ReLU approximate equality test. Inside the `1/embedding_step_sharpness`-wide transition ball around the target the output interpolates between -1 and +1.

### `equals_vector_match_and_off`

Half exact-match against target vector [1, 2, 3], half random vectors in [-3, 3]³ — measures the ReLU-based equality test's margin of separation between matches and non-matches.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x[0]=1`, `x[1]=2`, `x[2]=3` |

## `exp`

Natural exponential via piecewise-linear interpolation with uniform breakpoint spacing. Constant relative output error per cell `(Δx)²/8` because `d²exp/dx² = exp`. Pairs with `log` to implement `A·B = exp(log A + log B)` and `A/B = exp(log A − log B)` in log-space chains.

### `exp_pm5`

exp(x) over [-5, 5] — output spans [exp(-5), exp(5)] ≈ [0.0067, 148.4], the natural pairing for log over [0.01, 100]. Uniform 256-BP grid (`Δx ≈ 0.0392`); per-cell relative-error bound is `(Δx)²/8 ≈ 1.9e-4`.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.02797 |
| Mean abs error | 0.001716 |
| p99 abs error | 0.02094 |
| Max rel error | 0.0001924 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.0001204 |
| p99 rel error | 0.0001922 |
| Worst input | `x=4.98` |

## `floor_int`

Staircase with ramp width `1/step_sharpness` directly below each integer. Inputs near integer boundaries land in the ramp zone and produce interpolated values; callers with integer-ish inputs should use `thermometer_floor_div` instead, whose ramps sit at half-integer thresholds.

### `floor_near_boundary_10`

Samples within ±1/step_sharpness of integer k=5 — stresses the ramp zone where `floor_int` interpolates.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.9993 |
| Mean abs error | 0.1288 |
| p99 abs error | 0.9704 |
| Max rel error | 0.2498 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.03219 |
| p99 rel error | 0.2426 |
| Worst input | `x=5` |

### `floor_uniform_neg5_10`

Uniform continuous samples on [-5, 10] — most land in flat zones; measures baseline `floor_int` accuracy.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.9982 |
| Mean abs error | 0.04655 |
| p99 abs error | 0.8959 |
| Max rel error | 0.953 (over 3828 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.01849 |
| p99 rel error | 0.4611 |
| Worst input | `x=6` |

## `linear_bin_index`

End-to-end composite (~6 MLP sublayers). Error compounds across `reciprocal`, `signed_multiply`, `clamp`, and the terminal `thermometer_floor_div`. Inputs close to integer bin boundaries land in the staircase's ramp zone; `_bin_index_ref` uses `torch.floor` on the exact linear mapping, so tight bin edges reveal ramp-zone slippage.

### `linear_bin_index_tex_col_16`

16-bin quantisation of x ∈ [0, 64] with x_min=0, x_max=64.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 1 |
| Mean abs error | 0.06309 |
| p99 abs error | 0.6875 |
| Max rel error | 0.9854 (over 3715 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.01339 |
| p99 rel error | 0.2241 |
| Worst input | `x=60`, `x_min=0`, `x_max=64` |

## `log`

Natural log via per-section piecewise-linear interpolation. The op auto-sections the input range geometrically by `section_factor=10` (default decades) and routes via thermometer compare + multiply_2d blending, so float32 cancellation is bounded by section width regardless of overall input range. Pairs with `exp` for log-space arithmetic chains.

### `log_4decades_001_100`

log(x) over [0.01, 100] — 4 decades. With per-decade sectioning (default), each section's pre-cancellation magnitude is bounded by `section_factor=10`, so float32 ULP noise is ~1.2e-6 per section. The dominant residual error comes from the multiply_2d blending grid in routing.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.000247 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0001126 |
| p99 abs error | 0.0001694 |
| Max rel error | 0.003721 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 4.257e-05 |
| p99 rel error | 0.0002646 |
| Worst input | `x=0.01797` |

### `log_6decades_wide`

log(x) over [0.01, 30000] — 6 decades. Stresses the sectioning path: a single-section piecewise log over this range fails outright (cancellation floor `(x_max/x_min) · 2⁻²³ ≈ 0.36 absolute, observed worst ~1.0 abs). Sectioning drops the floor to ~5e-3 absolute, dominated by compare-cancellation noise at large `x` propagating through multiply_2d blending.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.003026 |
| Mean abs error | 0.001523 |
| p99 abs error | 0.002798 |
| Max rel error | 0.001149 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.0001697 |
| p99 rel error | 0.0003993 |
| Worst input | `x=2.707e+04` |

## `log_abs`

Fused `log(clamp(|x|, min_abs, max_abs))` for signed input. Single-piecewise V-shape over signed `x` for `max_abs/min_abs ≤ 10⁴` (1 sublayer); falls back to `abs + sectioned log` for wider ratios (3 sublayers). Pairs with `exp` and Linear addition for log-domain multiplication of a signed by a positive value.

### `log_abs_3decades_pm100`

log_abs(x) for x ∈ [-100, 100] with min_abs=0.1, max_abs=100 (3 decades on |x|). Signed log-uniform |x| samples plus a V-bottom grid concentrated in [-0.2, 0.2] to stress the flat-zone transition at ±min_abs. Single-piecewise path (ratio 1000 < single-piecewise threshold).

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.000699 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0002524 |
| p99 abs error | 0.0004621 |
| Max rel error | 0.1428 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.0006318 |
| p99 rel error | 0.007789 |
| Worst input | `x=87.15` |

## `low_rank_2d`

Rank-3 SVD of `atan(cross/dot)` on a non-uniform grid. Worst-cell error is bounded by σ_{K+1}, the first truncated singular value — verify against the SVD computed in `tests/ops/test_low_rank_2d.py:100`.

### `atan_cross_dot_nonuniform`

atan2(cross/dot) over [-2, 2] × [0.5, 10] on a non-uniform grid — rank-3 SVD via `low_rank_2d`.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.0651 |
| Mean abs error | 0.01586 |
| p99 abs error | 0.05744 |
| Max rel error | 0.3314 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.08032 |
| p99 rel error | 0.2172 |
| Worst input | `a=2`, `b=3.365` |

## `max`

Exact via `(a + b + |a - b|) / 2`.

### `minmax_uniform_pm50`

Two independent uniform streams on [-50, 50]². `min`/`max` are exact via the abs-trick; residual is float32 round-off only.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 3.815e-06 |
| Mean abs error | 5.318e-07 |
| p99 abs error | 3.815e-06 |

| Mean rel error | 6.585e-08 |
| p99 rel error | 1.953e-06 |
| Worst input | `a=-50`, `b=-46.83` |

## `min`

Exact via `(a + b - |a - b|) / 2`.

Two independent uniform streams on [-50, 50]². `min`/`max` are exact via the abs-trick; residual is float32 round-off only.

## `mod_const`

Identity `x % d = x - d · thermometer_floor_div(x, d)`. Exact on integer inputs (the input class this op is declared for).

### `mod_integers_0_100_by7`

Integer inputs on [0, 100] with divisor=7. `mod_const` is x − divisor × `thermometer_floor_div(x)`, exact on integers.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 3486 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=64` |

## `multiply_2d`

Single-sublayer product via 2D piecewise-linear lookup. Analytical worst-cell absolute bound is `step1*step2/4 = 0.25` for this configuration.

### `multiply_uniform_pm10`

a·b over [-10, 10]² with step=1 — generic uniform product grid; analytical bound `step1*step2/4 = 0.25`.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.0625 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0202 |
| p99 abs error | 0.05847 |
| Max rel error | 2.15 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.007293 |
| p99 rel error | 0.06618 |
| Worst input | `a=-1.746`, `b=-1.746` |

## `multiply_integers`

Polarization + `square` on non-negative integer inputs. Exact on integer inputs by construction (the polarization identity is exact; `square` is exact at integer grid points).

### `multiply_integers_0_10`

Integer pairs in [0, 10] — `multiply_integers` is exact on integer inputs by construction; serves as a precision-parity check.

## `piecewise_linear`

Foundation 1D piecewise-linear primitive; measured on the canonical x² test function with 11 integer breakpoints. Error is zero at breakpoints and bounded by the interpolation error of the reference function over each segment.

### `parabola_0_10_step1`

f(x)=x² on [0, 10] with integer breakpoints — probes generic `piecewise_linear` precision between grid points.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.25 |
| Mean abs error | 0.1638 |
| p99 abs error | 0.25 |
| Max rel error | 231.1 (over 4094 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.5851 |
| p99 rel error | 11.4 |
| Worst input | `x=8.5` |

## `piecewise_linear_2d`

Triangulated-grid lookup with non-uniform breakpoints. On non-uniform grids `piecewise_linear_2d` uses a constrained least-squares fit that can oscillate in cell interiors — `low_rank_2d` is preferred there.

### `diff_trig_nonuniform`

Two-input product over DIFF_BP × TRIG_BP — DIFF in [-40, 40] (non-uniform), TRIG in [-1, 1] (non-uniform).

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 7.778 |
| Mean abs error | 1.301 |
| p99 abs error | 6.543 |
| Max rel error | 0.6014 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.118 |
| p99 rel error | 0.35 |
| Worst input | `a=22.22`, `b=-1` |

Two-input product over DIFF_BP × VEL_BP — DIFF in [-40, 40] (non-uniform), VEL in [-0.7, 0.7] (non-uniform).

## `reciprocal`

Geometric breakpoint spacing keeps relative interpolation error roughly constant across the range. Measured against a reciprocal graph with `(min_value, max_value, step) = (0.3, 200.0, 1.0)`.

### `reciprocal_03_200`

1/x over [0.3, 200.0], step=1.0.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.0009137 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0001264 |
| p99 abs error | 0.0005805 |
| Max rel error | 0.1686 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.01649 |
| p99 rel error | 0.1027 |
| Worst input | `x=160.1` |

## `signed_multiply`

Polarization identity `a·b = (|a+b|² - |a-b|²)/4`. Absolute error scales with `step × (max_abs1 + max_abs2)`, not with `|a·b|`; pathological near-zero × large-magnitude inputs exhibit high relative error.

### `signed_multiply_pm10`

a·b over [-10, 10]² via polarization identity, step=1.0 — analytical bound `step × (max_abs1 + max_abs2) / 4 = 5.0` absolute, much smaller relative.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.06248 |
| Mean abs error | 0.0202 |
| p99 abs error | 0.05845 |
| Max rel error | 2.15 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.007293 |
| p99 rel error | 0.06615 |
| Worst input | `a=-1.746`, `b=-1.746` |

## `square`

Always an underestimate of x² between grid points. Max segment error is `(step/2)²` at half-integers.

### `square_unsigned_0_10`

x² over [0, 10] with step=1.0 — exact at integers, worst error at half-integers.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.25 |
| Mean abs error | 0.1638 |
| p99 abs error | 0.25 |
| Max rel error | 231.1 (over 4094 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.5851 |
| p99 rel error | 11.4 |
| Worst input | `x=8.5` |

## `square_signed`

Same interpolation profile as `square` but spans [-max_abs, max_abs]; saves one MLP sublayer vs. abs+square in `multiply_integers` / `signed_multiply` deep strategies.

### `square_signed_pm10`

x² over [-10, 10] with step=1.0 — covers the negative half the unsigned variant cannot reach.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0.25 |
| Mean abs error | 0.164 |
| p99 abs error | 0.25 |
| Max rel error | 276.7 (over 4096 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0.7489 |
| p99 rel error | 13.24 |
| Worst input | `x=7.502` |

## `thermometer_floor_div`

Integer-input-only staircase. Ramps sit at `k*divisor - 0.5`, giving clean separation for integer inputs. Feeding continuous floats produces ramp-zone interpolation artefacts — those belong to `floor_int`, not this op.

### `thermometer_integers_0_100_by10`

Integer inputs on [0, 100] with divisor=10 — `thermometer_floor_div` is defined only for integer inputs.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Samples | 4096 |
| Max abs error | 0 |
| Mean abs error | 0 |
| p99 abs error | 0 |
| Max rel error | 0 (over 3699 samples with &#124;ref&#124; ≥ 1e-6) |
| Mean rel error | 0 |
| p99 rel error | 0 |
| Worst input | `x=64` |

## Methodology

Measurements run through `torchwright.debug.noise.measure_op_isolated`,
which builds a minimal graph around the op and evaluates it via
`Node.compute` — the same oracle path the compiler already trusts as
the semantic definition of each node. The piecewise-linear approximation
error is the difference between that oracle evaluation and the exact-math
reference function declared in `_target_ops` at
`scripts/measure_op_noise.py`.

All runs are CPU-only and seeded so measurements are bit-identical across
machines at a given commit; the consistency test in `tests/docs/` relies
on that. Floats in the JSON are rounded to 6 significant figures; the
markdown and docstring footers display `.4g` formatting.

**Relative error** is reported alongside absolute error because absolute
numbers are misleading for ops whose output magnitude varies over orders of
magnitude (reciprocal, multiplication, low-rank lookups). Per-sample relative
error is `|compiled - reference| / |reference|`, computed only where
`|reference| >= 1e-6` — samples with a near-zero reference are excluded
(rel error is ill-defined there) and the count of contributing samples is
reported as `rel_valid_samples`. Ops whose reference is always near zero
show `n/a` for relative error.

## Adding a new op

1. In `scripts/measure_op_noise.py`, add any new `InputDistribution` to
   `_distributions()` with a production-grounded name.
2. Append a `TargetOp(...)` to `_target_ops()` wiring `build_graph`,
   `reference_fn`, `input_specs`, and `distribution_names`.
3. Run `make measure-noise`. Commit the diff in `docs/`,
   `torchwright/ops/`, and nothing else.
